class.name: MultiNodeMesh
class.brief_description: Renders a mesh for each instance using GPU-instanced MultiMesh.
class.description: MultiNodeMesh renders a shared mesh for every active instance in the parent MultiNode using a single MultiMesh draw call. Inactive instances are hidden via zero-scale transform. Supports per-instance color modulation and material overrides. This is the primary visual node for most use cases.

members.member: The mesh resource rendered for each instance. All instances share this single mesh. Assign any Mesh resource (BoxMesh, SphereMesh, imported .obj, etc.).
Optional material that overrides the mesh's built-in material for all instances. Leave empty to use the mesh's own material.

When enabled, per-instance colors from the MultiNodeVisual3D base class are passed to the MultiMesh. Your shader must read COLOR or INSTANCE_COLOR to use them. Enabling this allocates an extra 4 floats per instance in the GPU buffer.
